researched_category.value: Developer Tools / CLI for Diagram Generation
researched_category.notes: Mermaid CLI is a command-line tool that converts Mermaid diagram definitions (text-based Markdown-like syntax) into rendered images (SVG, PNG, PDF). It's an official CLI wrapper around the Mermaid.js library.

one_liner.value: Command-line interface for the Mermaid library that converts text-based diagram definitions into SVG, PNG, or PDF images.
one_liner.notes: The GitHub repository describes it as a 'command line tool for the Mermaid library' that 'takes a mermaid definition file as input and generates an SVG/PNG/PDF file as output'.

methods.notes: Mermaid CLI is a local CLI/Node.js tool that runs entirely on the user's machine. It has no authentication mechanism, no API keys, no OAuth - it simply reads input files and writes output files locally. It does not connect to any external service requiring auth.

access.notes: Mermaid CLI is MIT-licensed open source. It is freely available on npm (@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli) and Docker Hub (minlag/mermaid-cli). No account signup, payment, or approval of any kind is required to install and use it. A developer can simply run 'npm install -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli' and start using the 'mmdc' command.

summary.value: Mermaid CLI does not expose a REST or GraphQL API. Its public interface consists of (1) a CLI binary ('mmdc') that accepts input/output file paths and rendering options, and (2) a Node.js API ('run()' function) that can be imported directly into Node.js scripts. It uses Puppeteer headless browser under the hood to render diagrams. Documentation is in the GitHub README; there is no separate API reference site.
summary.notes: The GitHub README documents both the CLI usage (mmdc command) and the Node.js API (import { run } from '@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli'). No REST endpoints exist - this is purely a local tool.

agent_interface.notes: While Mermaid CLI itself is a local tool, Mermaid Chart Inc. (the same team behind Mermaid CLI) provides an official hosted MCP server at mcp.mermaid.ai that offers validate-and-render Mermaid diagram capabilities. The MCP server has both free (no auth) tools and authenticated tools for Mermaid Chart account integration. Additionally, Mermaid CLI can be used directly as a CLI command or Node.js API by agents.

rationale.value: Mermaid CLI is buildable into an agent toolkit today through multiple pathways: (1) Direct CLI invocation - agents with shell access can call 'mmdc' to generate diagrams; (2) Node.js API - agents running in Node.js environments can import and use the 'run()' function; (3) Official MCP Server - Mermaid Chart provides a hosted MCP server at mcp.mermaid.ai with tools like 'validate_and_render_mermaid_diagram' that require no authentication for basic use; (4) Community MCP servers - several community-built MCP servers wrap Mermaid CLI. No paid plans, API keys, or approval gates are needed for core functionality.
rationale.notes: The main consideration is that the CLI must be installed in the agent's environment (via npm or Docker). For remote agent use, the Mermaid Chart MCP server provides hosted endpoints. The local CLI approach has no rate limits or costs, while the MCP server has a free tier.

s1.quote: This is a command-line interface (CLI) for mermaid. It takes a mermaid definition file as input and generates an SVG/PNG/PDF file as output.
s1.title: mermaid-js/mermaid-cli - GitHub

s4.quote: Use Node.JS API: It's possible to call mermaid-cli via a Node.JS API. import { run } from '@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli'; await run('input.mmd', 'output.svg');
s4.title: mermaid-js/mermaid-cli - Node.js API documentation

s5.quote: The Mermaid MCP Server enables AI assistants to create, validate, and render Mermaid diagrams seamlessly... Core Validation & Rendering Tools (No Authentication Required): validate_and_render_mermaid_diagram
s5.title: MCP Server — Mermaid Chart

s6.quote: Official classification icon official... Validates and renders Mermaid diagrams through hosted endpoints
s6.title: Official Mermaid Chart MCP Server | PulseMCP

limitations: Mermaid CLI is a local tool - it has no hosted REST/GraphQL API of its own. The official MCP server is provided by Mermaid Chart (the commercial entity behind Mermaid), not the Mermaid CLI package itself. The Node.js API is explicitly noted as 'not covered by semver' in the documentation.
